textByHashes.89f73d673355c8a9e13ffbf16a38c36adc73c9df: Any time Arianwyn, how can I help?
textByHashes.8825ca47460d9219870c88a681230db4f1642308: Alright, hand it here and I shall take a look.
textByHashes.9ab29e800544e173a8a42c833d3f4166d0cee0bf: Now, let me see... Hmm... Ah, there we go.
textByHashes.daa08d2be42139da418ea6532985836c1e7bb814: No problem, Arianwyn will tell you what you should do with it. Now I had better get going, see you round.
textByHashes.8bec3cc7566b158865d23732599a3195133fe9db: Maybe this human isn't as bad as you think Islwyn
textByHashes.a694c8e26a30914c7ecd5f414795f1f3a3bb1544: That I do... It is also elvish tradition to plant a specially enchanted crystal seed at the graves of our ancestors. The seed will create guardians to protect the area. Unfortunately the crystal seed must be tuned to the person it's protecting... a new seed won't do. But you should be able to recover the seed from her old tomb. I'm not very familiar with the lands to the east so I couldn't tell you where her old tomb is I'm afraid.
textByHashes.ecc3d72b51bc272646110c3a63340fc4f58587c9: Excellent. Now, the tomb's guardians will be protecting the seed, you'll need to defeat one of them to get it. Once you have it, return to me and I will re-enchant it.
textByHashes.f6f78b3dd233ebbc8e9408db37f3f0616dd7e340: Hello, any luck finding the consecration seed?
textByHashes.08b8dc4f02de503316fa9c894456193b9456274d: You should be able to find it in her old resting place, I think you said it was south east of Baxtorian Falls. You'll need to defeat one of the old tomb's guardians to obtain it. Bring it to me as soon as you find it, then I can re-enchant it.
textByHashes.d79f79f0acead61ec12963db8835087bd71046a0: Plant this seed in Glarial's new tomb, close to her remains. With it in place, she will rest in peace.
textByHashes.84ee8a25ace487c2cfff23edc03f593198df3b81: Not to worry, now I know the harmonic of Glarial's crystal, I can reproduce another one.
textByHashes.1159d74069557a2473750bf6bc96d55bfe99a736: Hey, How's it going? Have you managed to reconsecrate Glarial's resting place?
textByHashes.83cd2cc4152faa975145f0d89364029dba8c4ecb: Well when you're ready, plant the seed in Glarial's new tomb, close to her remains. With it in place, she will rest in peace.
textByHashes.8052847fc4050cd20c439d7e57ee55a20086ee50: Well done. You should let Islwyn know.
textByHashes.e8881520e564df7d449f2a14c6f3a12b281a4548: Hi, I hear you finished consecrating Glarial's tomb. Well done.
textByHashes.1110b2000b2f272c26957bd41c6c0ffa23048504: Indeed it was. Anyway, are you ready to be taken to Arianwyn?
textByHashes.79f5385f8a46d2d7536fcd8f32bb73fafb7cc8b6: Hey there . Arianwyn's leading this meeting so you should let him know when you're ready to start.
textByHashes.492f5f27e9c333e90aaf2807dc91b1d0ade2071a: Seren? But that's impossible, Seren is gone.
textByHashes.46a4edae89d7e5a3327b76d109477206259ac8da: Seren was our goddess, our guardian and our teacher. Everything we have, we owe to her. She helped us build the great city of Prifddinas, a place where all elves could live in harmony.
textByHashes.86616c1c0ce458fbea17c6363a7f041c9d4fd32a: We always are. Good luck to you both.
textByHashes.35ba2a42cbe46a22118e516f25840de64d9187d5: You also missed out on Islwyn getting to meet his grandfather for the first time. I think he might have cried a little.
textByHashes.5f457783d83c8ade7db20fd4c390eb003b62b86e: A way to access the library?
textByHashes.fbdbfcec8f5fd69fae6fb76f6d104e72abf2ce4f: Hello again, how is it going?
textByHashes.ecee115cb70870df174bb6fcd1697fa966fca0a2: It's good to have you helping us. We're all very grateful.
textByHashes.3a5e92cc6f222d7cc6d8ad1857cf6c4147e852ae: Okay, let me see what I can do with it.
textByHashes.05e922879641a06c1ce3da029061ed17beb9f13b: There you go. Be careful with it, I'm not sure what it will do when used.
textByHashes.5efe605ea9c7d4ab7663850fb8485afce75eec78: So we have a way to bypass the defences?
textByHashes.e8ef843920ce96bd3b76efb830a17d0503ea5025: Of course, the crystal seeds. All of us carry them in some form or other, and all of them came from Seren when she shattered herself.
textByHashes.bad408f87bf03b03146e1b565b2d1d5d4a82bc1d: Then he might already have claimed it. We should send someone to check.
textByHashes.d36e0826833cc18447655c473fe1f2d32a56adf6: You're not the only one. I think we all need time to process this.
textByHashes.c15df27c429d19e89661eaf31297432e9908415b: But why would Lord Iorwerth keep the orb at the camp? Surely it would be more secure behind the Prifddinas walls.
textByHashes.87bbcfce29be065ffd07bb19710ea20bd1e00924: But this fragment of Seren, it's still part of her. Lord Iorwerth has done some unspeakable things, but he doesn't serve Zamorak like we thought, maybe we can reason with him.
textByHashes.d5f81988d5cd544545472e24207c03a976b211d1: So what do we do? Baxtorian?
textByHashes.b4d89e5ff1372798e8fe0aaaae7476b7e5443071: Arianwyn is out there, walking alongside our greatest enemy.
textByHashes.29b2e1d8ef40e0d13fcfae933f12dc93d01e9275: He saved me once you know. Some of Lord Iorwerth's men found me in the forest. There were too many for me to fight off, I thought I was done for. Without Arianwyn, I wouldn't be here today.
textByHashes.e5396c6cd90829eb08cce2d536034deed776fb9c: But now he fights alongside them. This war has torn us apart, all of us.
textByHashes.a12bcaaf5cd349e37c69cebbed7798f839911090: Yes, today it ends... For better, or for worse.
textByHashes.3adc7765140606e627d6237d5fca6c4c6ce09e5d: Don't worry, I'll take care of her.
textByHashes.290b52f401e28d3f3f59b97c29129d49006a25bf: You don't seem to have any crystals I can enchant. Return when you do.
textByHashes.1cfd49dc393970b1362e1d1b07177784a1ae5769: Hey , do you need me to re-enchant your teleport crystal?
textByHashes.062d4a9b680357753fc41728ede708b2b3f8e1c2: I ask coins to cover my costs.
textByHashes.2d27127a382df9a274289420d5353865c38ccefa: I need you to answer this for me.
textByHashes.a5873afde24b6e634209fe8b166fc26b67a987ec: Please help me out! How much are they worth?
textByHashes.d4b12bb58e5de9d4949e143ad973d433223a738d: How can that be? Go check again!
